title: Biggest questions, projections and more: Storylines to watch ahead of NBA playoffs
content: Our NBA insiders answer the biggest questions facing contenders in the East and West as the regular season winds to a close.

title: Sources: Giannis refuses Bucks' shutdown pitch
content: The Bucks want to shut down Giannis Antetokounmpo for the remainder of the season after his latest injury and their postseason chances dwindled, but Antetokounmpo has refused the team's requests and wants to play again, sources told ESPN.

title: Sources: Eagles trading for Panthers QB Dalton
content: The Panthers are trading veteran quarterback Andy Dalton to the Eagles in exchange for a 2027 seventh-round pick, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: HOF won't give puck to Hughes: Not his to own
content: The Hockey Hall of Fame said it will not give Jack Hughes the puck from his gold-medal-winning goal at the Milan Cortina Olympics.

title: USC lands 3rd McDonald's All American for 2026
content: Top-five senior Christian Collins announced his commitment to USC on Wednesday, giving the Trojans their third McDonald's All American in the 2026 class.

title: Sources: New WNBA CBA includes $7M salary cap
content: After a prolonged labor battle, the WNBA and players' union have reached a verbal agreement on the terms for a new CBA. Sources told ESPN that the salary cap will start at $7 million and raise the average salary to around $600,000.

title: Duke likely without Ngongba for tourney opener
content: Duke will open the NCAA tournament Thursday without big man Patrick Ngongba, who continues to recover from a foot injury, putting some added pressure on star freshman Cameron Boozer.

title: Rory spares no expense with Masters dinner menu
content: Rory McIlroy hosts this year's Champions Dinner at the Masters, with his menu including wagyu filet mignon, seared salmon, glazed carrots and Vidalia onion rings.

title: Sources: Dolphins star Achane off-limits in trade
content: The Dolphins have informed inquiring teams that De'Von Achane is not available in a trade, sources told ESPN.

content: There was one clear favorite among the 25 coaches polled. Here are their takes on seven contenders and more.

content: Who are the top players in the women's NCAA bracket? Where do Azzi Fudd, Hannah Hidalgo and Lauren Betts rank?

content: One must-see guard scores from anywhere like Kelsey Plum. Another handles pressure like Chelsea Gray.

content: Beers has never had alcohol, but she seized the opportunity to capitalize on her name when her jersey went viral.

content: Denied gold by a Venezuelan team that symbolized the event at its best, Bryce Harper and the U.S. couldn't help but relish in the beauty of the WBC.

title: WNBA CBA negotiations: At last, WNBA and union 'aligned' on new CBA
content: The salary cap of the new CBA will reportedly start at $7 million, with the supermax salary starting at $1.4 million.

content: Pittsburgh faces off against Carolina on Wednesday. Plus, full playoff projections, draft lottery standings after a busy Tuesday.

content: Sporting became only the fifth club in Champions League history to win a two-legged knockout tie despite losing the first leg by three goals or more.

content: Tuesday's 3-0 defeat to PSG in the Champions League showed just how far off the pace the Blues really are.

content: Which double-digit seeds have the highest potential to advance to the second round? Here's what ESPN's BPI says.

content: Will Aaron Rodgers check the QB box for the Steelers? Can the Seahawks find running back help? And is there something the Chiefs can do at cornerback?

title: As Premier League sides crumble around them, Arsenal keep cruising closer to a quadruple
content: Arsenal should feel the pressure as Premier League leaders. Instead, they look comfortable as a quadruple is in their sights.

title: Jackson Chourio, Roman Anthony among must-draft players in fantasy baseball
content: Which players are Tristan H. Cockcroft higher on than most? Here's a list of 20 names to keep in mind when drafting your 2026 rosters.

title: US spy chief says Iran not rebuilding nukes, undercutting Trump’s war rationale
content: US intelligence indicates Iran has not rebuilt nuclear enrichment capacity post-strikes, contradicting President Trump's war justification. Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard's testimony revealed no efforts to restore capability, despite Trump's claims of an imminent threat. This finding fuels Democratic pressure regarding the administration's rationale for the ongoing conflict.

title: Nvidia’s Jensen Huang become first CEO to roll out token budgets for recruitment
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang proposed offering engineers annual "token budgets" as part of their compensation, potentially amounting to half their base salary. This move aims to boost engineer productivity and serve as a new recruiting tool in Silicon Valley. Huang believes this will amplify engineers' capabilities, making them significantly more effective.

title: US Fed keeps interest rates unchanged; flags 'uncertainty' amid Iran war
content: Experts had said that fears of oil prices stoking inflation were unlikely to push the Federal Reserve toward raising interest rates at this week’s meeting. hile markets and economists had earlier anticipated that the next move would be a rate cut, that conviction has weakened since the United States and Israel launched airstrikes on Iran on February 28.

title: Ugadi: Wishes, quotes, images, rangoli designs, facebook & Whatsapp status to share
content: Ugadi, a festival marking the new year in South India, emphasizes a quiet reset rather than grand celebrations. It's about acknowledging life's mix of experiences, from sweet to bitter, and preparing for the year ahead with simple wishes and personal reflection. Modern celebrations blend online greetings with traditional practices, focusing on mindfulness and genuine connection.

title: Sam Altman sends 'thanks note' to engineers with the message that 'we plan to take your job'
content: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman expressed gratitude to early software engineers, acknowledging their complex work. However, his comments sparked debate about AI's impact on coding jobs, with users interpreting his message as a prelude to displacement. This follows recent workforce reductions and AI investment announcements from companies like Block Inc. and Atlassian, signaling a broader trend of automation affecting white-collar roles.

title: US-Iran war: Nvidia CEO Huang shares personal message for employees in Israel
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang conveyed deep concern for his 6,000 Israeli employees and their families amidst the ongoing conflict, assuring them of the company's unwavering support and long-term commitment to Israel.  He highlighted the critical role of Israeli talent in developing key Nvidia technologies, underscoring the nation's importance to the company's innovation pipeline and future expansion plans.

title: Labelled 'traitors', Iran women's football team return home after asylum episode
content: Iranian media on Wednesday showed visuals of the players arriving in the country via Turkey, where they landed before travelling by bus to the border, news agency AP reported. Officials received them upon arrival.

title: Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2: Release date and time revealed
content: Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 launches March 19, 2026, at 4:00 AM ET, concluding the longest season to date. This major update (v40.00) introduces a revamped battle royale map with new locations like Ice King's castle, an overhauled loot pool, and story content featuring The Seven. A new Battle Pass will also be available, including a Bugs Bunny skin.

title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce reveal scary reality before wedding
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are no strangers to performing under pressure, regularly facing crowds of more than 70,000 people. Kelce recently admitted that the adrenaline of rising to the occasion in packed stadiums is something he will deeply miss after retirement. Swift experiences similar intensity during her Eras Tour, proving that performing before massive audiences can inspire excellence rather than fear.

title: NFL Mock Draft 2026: Vikings eye Harrison Smith replacement
content: The Minnesota Vikings are preparing for a post-June 1 release of veteran safety Harrison Smith. This has prompted speculation about his successor. Analysts are projecting Oregon Ducks safety Dillon Thieneman as a strong candidate. It is his football IQ and athleticism that stand out. However, some mock drafts suggest Thieneman might be a better fit for the Sam Darnold’s Seattle Seahawks. Especially for the head coach Mike Macdonald’s two-high safety scheme.

title: Zakat al Mal vs Zakat al Fitr: Key difference many Muslims still get wrong ahead of Eid
content: As Ramadan 2026 concludes, a key question arises: the distinction between Zakat al Mal and Zakat al Fitr. While both are mandatory Islamic charities, Zakat al Mal is an annual wealth-based obligation, whereas Zakat al Fitr is a fixed per-person charity due before Eid al Fitr prayer, ensuring inclusion for all.

title: Horoscope : Astrological predictions for March 19
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for March 19, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: NHL player safety department imposes fine on Hurricanes Forward Seth Jarvis
content: Seth Jarvis of the Carolina Hurricanes has been fined five thousand dollars by the NHL Player Safety Department. The fine is for a high-sticking incident involving Conor Garland of the Columbus Blue Jackets. The incident occurred during a game on Tuesday night. Garland suffered a facial injury and required medical attention. Jarvis received a double-minor penalty for the infraction.

title: Operation 'Epic Failure': Is the Iran war backfiring on Trump?
content: While US and Israeli forces have inflicted significant damage on Iran's military and leadership, the conflict has escalated into a regional confrontation. Iran's strategy of prolonging the war and disrupting global markets poses a challenge to President Trump's ability to achieve a decisive political victory, raising concerns about the war's long-term consequences and potential for isolation.

title: Smith says missing IPL a blessing in disguise ahead of Tests
content: Smith, who went unsold in the IPL auction, said he initially wanted to be part of the league but now sees the break as useful ahead of England’s Test summer, which begins against New Zealand in June.

title: Pakistan, China pose biggest nuclear threat to US, says Tulsi Gabbard
content: US intelligence chief Tulsi Gabbard identified Pakistan as a major nuclear threat, alongside Iran, China, Russia, and North Korea. These nations are developing advanced missile systems capable of reaching the US homeland.  Gabbard also noted North Korea's growing ties with Russia and China, while stating Iran's nuclear program was effectively halted last June.

title: 'Always fighting': Ab de Villiers explains Virat Kohli's influence in RCB
content: RCB will begin their IPL 2026 campaign on March 28 against Sunrisers Hyderabad at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru. The team will enter the season after winning their first title last year.

title: Candace Owens draws criticism over women remark as Erika Kirk series pause impacts YouTube numbers
content: Candace Owens claimed her podcast is number one globally, but users on X questioned the data. She replied that a 10-day break to prepare for her Erika Kirk series affected average numbers, not total reach. The exchange has drawn attention to how podcast rankings are calculated and put focus on her upcoming series.

title: Retirement soon? Buttler makes honest admission after poor T20 WC
content: Jos Buttler admitted he had a poor T20 World Cup, scoring just 87 runs, but remains determined to continue his England career. He said, "Obviously I had a poor tournament, which is disappointing," yet added he hopes to return to form. After taking a refreshing break in France, Buttler reflected, "It wasn’t for a lack of effort, it just didn't quite happen."

title: 'Wish my younger self had studied more': Former World Champion Liren
content: Former world champion Ding Liren has dropped out of the FIDE ratings list due to inactivity but says he is content with the break. After losing his title to D Gukesh, Ding is enjoying a quieter phase, playing casual online games. Reflecting on his journey, he admitted regrets about academics but remains relaxed, focusing on a less intense relationship with chess.

title: ‘I belong to people’: Vijay rejects alliance rumours, says no compromise on secular stand
content: Reiterating the party’s ideological position, Vijay said, “We will never compromise on our stand of secularism and social justice.”

title: Aditya Dhar apologises as Dhurandhar 2 premieres delayed, shows cancelled
content: Even as Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge finally began rolling out in cinemas, the much-hyped release hit an unexpected roadblock. Several premiere shows across regions were either delayed or cancelled, leaving fans disappointed. Kannada and Malayalam versions, in particular, did not screen as planned on opening evening.

title: X outage: Elon Musk's Twitter goes down, thousands affected
content: X, formerly Twitter, experienced a significant global outage on Wednesday, March 18, leaving users in India and the US unable to access the platform. The downtime, which began around 8:00 pm, saw thousands of incident reports on Downdetector, with a substantial number originating from India. Users reported issues with both the app and website, with some encountering error messages.

title: Govt sends additional warships to Hormuz to ensure safe passage of Indian vessels
content: India has deployed additional naval assets in the Arabian Sea and Gulf of Oman to ensure safe passage for its fuel-carrying vessels amid escalating tensions around the Strait of Hormuz. The Indian Navy's precautionary deployment aims to escort Indian ships to safer waters, as the strait's closure disrupts crucial energy imports for India.

title: Deion Sanders and Karrueche Tran spark 'gold digger' debate as viral video divides fans
content: Deion Sanders and Karrueche Tran's recent video has divided internet users. Some praise their bond after Sanders' 2025 health issues. Others question Tran's motives, citing the age gap and Sanders' wealth. Supporters highlight Tran's presence during his surgeries and recovery. The couple appears unfazed by public opinion as they navigate their relationship.

title: I-PAC raid row: SC questions Mamata's role, tells Bengal govt ED can't be left remedy-less
content: The Supreme Court has strongly criticized the West Bengal government's challenge to the Enforcement Directorate's plea regarding alleged obstruction by Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee during a raid. Justices questioned where the ED would seek recourse if not in courts, emphasizing no entity should lack remedies. The court also raised constitutional concerns over the alleged obstruction.

title: Eid truce: Pakistan and Afghanistan temporarily halt military operations after deadly Kabul strike
content: Pakistan has temporarily halted military operations against Afghanistan for Eid, following requests from Turkey, Saudi Arabia, and Qatar. This pause comes amid escalating border tensions after a deadly Kabul attack, which Pakistan claims targeted a site used for drone storage and training suicide bombers.

title: 'Ok if Gambhir wanted me, Kohli & Rohit out': Ashwin backs India coach's call
content: Ravichandran Ashwin explained his retirement decision, stating he wanted to leave on his own terms after feeling his time was up. He also backed head coach Gautam Gambhir, emphasizing that the coach has the right to make team-oriented decisions, even if it means senior players like himself, Kohli, or Rohit must move on.

title: Trump suspends Jones Act for 60 days as Iran war drives up US fuel costs - what it means
content: In a bid to curb soaring energy costs, President Trump has temporarily waived the Jones Act for 60 days. This move permits foreign ships to transport goods between US ports, aiming to ease oil market disruptions caused by the US-Israeli conflict with Iran.

title: 'Abusive relationship': Deve Gowda responds to Kharge's 'loved us, married Modiji' dig
content: Former Prime Minister HD Deve Gowda responded to Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge's remarks by calling his alliance with the Congress a "forced marriage" that ended in an "abusive relationship.

title: Novak Djokovic raises fitness questions after Miami open exit
content: Novak Djokovic withdrew from the Miami Open due to a shoulder issue, raising concerns about his match readiness. Martina Navratilova said he needs more matches before big tournaments. Djokovic has played limited matches this season despite strong results. With Monte Carlo Masters next, his fitness and form will be closely watched ahead of upcoming Grand Slams.

title: Rain lashes several parts of Delhi-NCR; thunderstorms, gusty winds likely this week, says IMD
content: Delhi-NCR experienced rainfall and gusty winds on Wednesday, causing a dip in temperatures and power outages. The India Meteorological Department forecasts continued light rain, thunderstorms, and strong winds until March 20, with temperatures expected to remain below normal. Residents are urged to take precautions during the adverse weather.

title: AFCON chaos: Senegal allege 'corruption' and reveal their next move
content: Senegal’s AFCON win has been overturned by Confederation of African Football, awarding the title to Morocco and sparking outrage. CAF cited rules after Senegal players briefly left the pitch during the final. Senegal has rejected the decision, alleged corruption, and will appeal to the Court of Arbitration for Sport, leaving the final outcome uncertain.

title: Amid LPG crisis, demand for rocket stoves surges in Kerala
content: Rocket stoves are high in demand as they require less firewood compared to regular wood stoves; as orders surge, stove suppliers have stopped accepting fresh orders

title: Ambulance drivers sensitised to Model Code of Conduct rules in Vellore, nearby districts
content: The guidelines say ambulance drivers should cooperate with Static Surveillance Teams during vehicle checks

title: Modi speaks to Kuwait Crown Prince; says safe, free navigation through Strait of Hormuz top priority
content: In their telephonic conversation, the two leaders agree that sustained diplomatic engagement remains essential for regional peace and stability

title: Assam Congress weighs MP’s flight to BJP
content: The fate of Pradyut Bordoloi’s son, the Congress candidate from eastern Assam’s Margherita constituency, hangs in the balance

title: 22 India-bound ships on Hormuz evacuation list
content: Of these, 20 vessels have been assessed as critical to India’s energy security, carrying over 5 lakh tonnes of LNG and LPG and 16.76 lakh tonnes of crude oil; various agencies join hands for evacuation

title: Lok Sabha ‘guillotines’ Demands for Grants worth ₹53 lakh crore
content: Union Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan says Modi government ‘committed to transform society, improving lives, and shaping the future of the nation’

title: Karnataka Information Commission brings Apex Bank under RTI Act
content: As the State Co-op Apex Bank has now now declared as public authority, the information relating to its functioning including financial decisions, will now be subject to public scrutiny under RTI

title: India sustains progress in reducing child deaths amid slowing global gains, says new UN report
content: The region’s under-five mortality has fallen significantly — from 92 deaths per 1,000 live births in 2000 to nearly 32 in 2024, reflecting sustained progress in child health outcomes, the Union Health Ministry says

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Congress candidate selection in limbo
content: After several rounds of meetings and consultations, central election committee of the party could not resolve the issue of allowing MPs from the State to contest the Assembly elections

title: Tribal Affairs Minister meets Vietnam counterpart to discuss Memorandum of Cooperation
content: Draft memorandum approved; Tribal Affairs Minister invited to Vietnam to witness signing of final agreement; collaboration potential in livelihood promotion, value addition to forest products, sustainable agriculture, skill development, research partnerships, and cultural documentation.

title: Are publication of books, manufacture of prasadam by temples an industrial activity, Supreme Court asks
content: The question was posed by Justice B.V. Nagarathna, who is part of a nine-judge Bench headed by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant examining the ambit of the definition of ‘industry’ and whether it would include charities and hospitals

title: China-bound Russian oil tanker diverted to India
content: Aqua Titan carrying 7.7 lakh barrels of crude makes U-turn in Southeast Asia, heading towards Mangalore port; analyst says ship part of the ‘dark fleet’ mostly controlled by the Russian state

title: Congress says Pradyut Bordoloi’s exit ‘unfortunate’, accuses BJP of poaching its leaders
content: Priyanka Gandhi Vadra says differences over ticket allocation may have triggered Bordoloi’s exit. MP Manickam Tagore says a traitor who switches sides in the middle of the battlefield cannot escape the judgment of the people.

title: 2-3 drones sighted along India’s border every day: Parliament panel report
content: As many as 967 cases of drone incursions occurred, which were intercepted or neutralised, resulting in the seizure of 710 ammunitions, 75 arms and 641 kg of drugs, the MHA has said

title: Second edition of IOS SAGAR set to boost maritime cooperation in Indian Ocean Region
content: Designed as a unique operational engagement programme, IOS SAGAR enables naval personnel from friendly foreign countries to train and sail together onboard an Indian Naval ship

title: Gandhi Bunakar mela opens in Vijayawada
content: The national-level handloom exhibition aimed at supporting handloom weavers and promoting traditional textiles will continue till March 31

title: Where’s the Minister, asks Speaker; says Question Hour more important than meetings
content: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla also urges MPs not to chat when the House takes up obituary references, says it is not a good practice

title: BJP urges traditional Ugadi celebrations to strengthen community bonds
content: Visit prominent temples in villages across districts and actively participate in the celebrations along with local communities, says party State president

title: Ugadi festivities at Kanaka Durga temple to commence on March 19
content: In view of heavy rush during weekends and festival days, new parking regulations have been introduced as per the directions of the State Home Department

title: Sri Lanka ready to welcome and support refugees from India: Minister Bimal Rathnayake
content: The Cabinet Minister requests Governments of India and Tamil Nadu to refrain from using them as ‘political propaganda tool’

title: Expelled Trinamool leader Humayun Kabir's party to contest 182 seats in West Bengal
content: The newly floated Aam Janata Unnayan Party will field a Muslim candidate against Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee in the high-profile Bhabanipur constituency

title: Qatar Says "Extensive Damage" Caused By Iran Attacks On Gas Facility
content: Qatar's state-run energy company said on Wednesday that fires set by Iranian strikes on its main gas facility had caused "extensive damage", after Tehran said it would retaliate in kind for an attack on its own energy infrastructure.

title: Iran War Live Updates: Iran Confirms Israel Killed Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib
content: US-Israel-Iran War News LIVE Updates: The war in the Middle East broke out when the US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28.

title: Air India To Operate 48 Middle East Flights Today, Dubai Services To Resume
content: Air India, Air India Express and IndiGo on Wednesday decided to resume services to Dubai while services to a few other cities in the region remain suspended.

title: China, Pak Developing Nuclear Missiles That Put US Within Range: Intel Chief
content: US Intelligence Chief Tulsi Gabbard, presenting the intelligence community's 2026 Annual Threat Assessment, said that Russia, China, North Korea, Iran and Pakistan are the most significant nuclear threats to the United States.

title: US Eases Venezuela Oil Sanctions To Allow Trade As Iran War Drives Up Prices
content: US companies will be allowed to do business with Venezuela's state-owned oil and gas company after the Treasury Department eased sanctions, with some limitations, on Wednesday.

title: 'Iran Wasn't Restarting Nuclear Program': US Intel Chief Contradicts Trump
content: US intelligence concluded Wednesday that Iran was not rebuilding nuclear enrichment capacities destroyed last year by the United States and Israel, contradicting a key justification by President Donald Trump for his ongoing war.

title: Pakistan, Afghanistan Announce 'Temporary Pause' In Fighting Due To Eid
content: Pakistan and Afghanistan on Wednesday announced a "temporary pause" in fighting in view of Eid-ul-Fitr and at the request of Saudi Arabia, Turkey and Qatar.

title: UP Man Kills 4-Year-Old Neighbour, Arrested After Encounter
content: A man accused of killing a 4-year-old girl in the Nandgram area here was arrested after an encounter in the early hours of Wednesday, officials said.

title: Kim Jong Un Near-Perfect Win In North Korea Polls With 99.93% Mandate
content: In North Korea's 2026 parliamentary elections, Kim Jong Un achieved a landslide win as the Workers' Party of Korea and its coalition partners reportedly captured 99.93 per cent of the ballot and every available seat, according to Yonhap News Agency.

title: 'Only Simple Flag-Hoisting': Pak Cancels Republic Day Parade Amid Oil Crisis
content: Pakistan has cancelled its military parade and ceremonial events that were scheduled for March 23, the country's Republic Day. Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif's office announced on Tuesday.

title: Larijani's Visit To Daughter's House Sealed His Fate. How He Was Located
content: Iran's security chief Ali Larijani's killing deals a major blow to Tehran's power structure as it retaliates to US-Israeli attacks amid a war that has pushed the Middle East into fresh chaos.

title: Gulmarg Land Lease Case Closed, Hoteliers To Opt For Government Settlement
content: The Jammu and Kashmir High Court has closed the Gulmarg land lease case after a mass withdrawal of petitions.

title: Dubai Real Estate Shows Resilience Amid Israel-Iran War
content: Several industries in the UAE are facing the impact of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, especially the hospitality, tourism and trading sectors.

title: Mumbai Constable Dies By Suicide, Alleges Harassment By Lover's Parents
content: A 32-year-old police constable allegedly died by suicide at the Kalina Police Headquarters in Mumbai. The man, identified as Bhaiyasaheb Bhimrao Wavale, was a native of Parbhani and had joined the Mumbai Police Force as a driver on February 7, 2026.

title: "Every Drop Of Blood Has A Price": Mojtaba Vows To Avenge Larijani Killing
content: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has vowed revenge for the killing of security chief Ali Larijani in a US-Israeli strike on Tuesday.

title: Israel-Iran War Hits Dubai's Hospitality Sector, Tourism Drops Sharply
content: The impact of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East is now clearly visible in the UAE, especially in Dubai, one of the region's biggest hubs for tourism, trade, and finance.

title: Centre Informed Panel 1,000 Indian Students Are Yet To Leave Iran: Sources
content: The government has stated that around 1,000 Indian students will leave Iran amid the ongoing war, sources have said, citing discussions in a parliamentary committee meeting.

title: Adani Ports' Colombo Terminal Handles Over 1 Million Containers In 1st Year
content: A rapid first-year ramp-up at the Port of Colombo in Sri Lanka underscores the expanding global footprint of India's Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Ltd.

title: Staff Shortages Cloud Madhya Pradesh's Farmer Welfare Push
content: While the Madhya Pradesh government is observing 2026 as the "Farmers' Welfare Year" with goals to increase rural incomes, the state's Agriculture Department is currently operating with a significant staff shortage.

title: 8 Killed, 97% Shipping Drop: Middle East War Chokes Hormuz Strait
content: The war erupted on February 28 when the US and Israel began bombing Iran, prompting it to retaliate with strikes across the region and restrict access to the strait.

title: Men's tournament: Best bets for the round of 64, plus Eliminator picks
content: As you prepare for tipoff, here are our best bets for the first round, plus picks for the new Eliminator game.

title: In final window before World Cup, Pochettino's USMNT roster shows softened stance on club form
content: Mauricio Pochettino once demanded peak club performance from his USMNT players, but his March squad raises the question of whether that's negotiable.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Liverpool, Man United want Wolves' João Gomes
content: Wolves midfielder João Gomes is a target for Liverpool and Manchester United. Join us for the latest transfer news and rumors from around the globe.

title: 'I salute every boxer out there' -- Lamar Jackson boxes in offseason workout
content: Jackson is looking to bounce back after a hamstring injury which saw him play in only 13 games last season.

title: Women's boxing pound-for-pound rankings: Gabriela Fundora's dominance fuels her rise
content: Gabriela Fundora delivers another KO to break into the top three. Here are the latest women's pound-for-pound rankings.

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: IUML bets on youth, ushers in generational shift
content: Breaking from expectations of retaining most of its 15 sitting MLAs, whose average age is above 63, the party introduced 11 new faces, including six young leaders, two women and one non-Muslim candidate

title: Why MGR’s election from Andipatti was challenged in the court?
content: The petitions did not question the polling or counting process. Instead, they raised a legal issue: whether MGR was constitutionally qualified to contest at all, in view of how and before whom he had made and subscribed the oath or affirmation mandated under Article 173 (a) of the Constitution.

title: Complete repair work of elephant trenches and hanging solar fences in Kodagu: Khandre
content: The Minister notes that 38% of deaths caused by human-wildlife conflict in the State occur in Kodagu district and directs officials to increase patrolling

title: Thirupparankundram row: ‘I cannot help feeling I have been taken for a ride,’ says Justice Swaminathan
content: The judge adjourns till April 9 the hearing on contempt of court cases initiated by him in connection with the row, following an interim stay granted by a Division Bench of the Madurai Bench of the Madras High Court

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: 10 of the 13 incumbent MLAs in Kozhikode contesting again; no woman candidate from CPI(M)
content: The Left Democratic Front had 11 MLAs and the United Democratic Front had two elected representatives in Kozhikode after the 2021 elections

title: 140 ex-Maoists visit Chhattisgarh Assembly, meet CM Sai
content: The CM emphasised that by placing their trust in the Constitution, the former Maoists have set a positive and inspiring example for others

title: Parking shed collapse at Prabuddha Academy raises safety concerns
content: This the second such incident that has occurred in the building after the roof of the dining hall collapsed about two weeks ago

title: Bihar Congress asks three truant MLAs to explain their absence during the RS polling
content: Their absence led to the defeat of the Mahagathbandhan candidate; the three MLAs alleged that the State Congress chief told them to skip voting as he had not been involved in choosing the alliance candidate

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026:  Shifting loyalties keep parties on tenterhooks in Koduvally
content: Koduvally, which was once a safe constituency of the United Democratic Front, is no longer a comfort zone for any front now, with changing loyalties and tactical battles altering its political landscape

title: Government seeks BJP’s support to ban DJ music during festivals
content: Minister for Kannada and Culture and Backward Classes Shivaraj Tangadagi said the government will support the proposal and that he will act if the BJP extends its cooperation.

title: District gears up for another flawless Thrissur Pooram
content: Collector chairs high-level review meeting; round-the-clock control rooms, safety, and crowd management take centre stage

title: Food and Civil Supplies Minister asks hotels to adjust operations for a week amid LPG shortage
content: We have barely about 1,000 cylinders for commercial use; it is difficult to decide how to distribute these, K.H. Muniyappa says

title: Kerala revises grace marks for students
content: Order does not mention grace marks for reading, as announced earlier by Minister for General Education V. Sivankutty

title: House panel flags vacancies in UGC, AICTE, says situation ‘critical’
content: The panel said “516 out of 763 sanctioned non-teaching positions are vacant”, amounting to a vacancy rate of 67.6%; 63.6% of posts in middle and senior administrative levels were vacant in AICTE

title: Luggage deposit, delivery centres come in handy for devotees in Tirumala
content: 80 counters are operational in Tirumala and along the pedestrian pathways, manned by 394 personnel working in three shifts to provide round-the-clock services

title: Government urged to address issues faced by shrimp farmers
content: Andhra Pradesh Farmers Association raise concerns over the rising cost of feed and the declining prices of shrimp

title: HRD Minister Lokesh promises job calendar every Ugadi
content: Insisting on a structured approach to employment generation, the HRD Minister asserts that large-scale employment will also come through the private sector investment, with efforts underway to attract multiple companies to Andhra Pradesh

title: In the Rajya Sabha, BJP MP calls for implementing OBC sub-quota for Central schemes
content: Sub-categorisation of OBCs has been carried out by various State governments for a while now, and recognises the graded backwardness of communities within a given categorisation

title: Kerala HC rejects PIL that challenged use of name ‘IPL’
content: Court observes that there was no merit in the substance of the argument and refuses to entertain the PIL

title: Iran war leads to drop in cargo volumes in Kerala ports
content: Cochin Port, only port in Kerala that handles crude oil and liquefied petroleum gas consignments, has witnessed a 20-30% drop in cargo volumes since the conflict began

title: Meta moves Delhi HC against CCPA fine for walkie-talkie sale on Facebook
content: It argued that the Central Consumer Protection Authority had no jurisdiction over it as Facebook was not an e-commerce platform but only a notice board

title: Amaravati allocates land for 109 institutions to boost capital development
content: Education sector receives the largest share of land, with 18 institutions allotted approximately 838.66 acres

title: Andhra Pradesh High Court reserves judgment on former MLA’s plea to transfer Amaravati IRR case to CBI
content: Chandrababu Naidu is one of the prime accused in the case; Judge reserves verdict in two other petitions, including one seeking cancellation of interim bail given to Naidu

title: Will Ferrell named as a Los Angeles World Cup host ambassador
content: the 2026 world cup community in los Angeles will be in good hands courtesy of the newest host ambassadors . will Ferrell will join 1999 women's world cup champions Julie Foudy . the host committee announced that he will be the community ambassador for the event .

title: Women's tournament odds: UConn odds-on favorite; UCLA, Texas, South Carolina next
content: the shortest odds for a pre-tournament favorite since 2018, was also the last time UConn was favored going into the tournament . the SEC dominates the next four spots between Texas (+700), south Carolina (+800), LSU (16-1, opened 14-1) and Vanderbilt (50-1) and Michigan (80-1) .

title: Barack Obama, Anthony Edwards shoot hoops, talk trash in Presidential Center opening tease

title: Week 5: Top 25 rankings, top moments and what to watch
content: the SEC got off to a fast and hot start, with Arkansas taking its home series against Mississippi State in Fayetteville and Texas finally losing its first game of the season . the Tigers dropped two games on the road to Vanderbilt . this week's biggest riser is crosstown rival USC, which rose up 12 spots .

title: Week 6 Top 25 and how to watch
content: the longhorns earned seven first-place votes, but still sit behind Tennessee . the rest of the top 10 remained the same, though it surely won't next week . there were no newcomers to the poll .

title: Broncos are all-in, and Dolphins are all-out, but does either strategy make sense?
content: the Broncos traded their first-, third- and fourth-round picks in this year's draft . they traded a wideout for Jaylen Waddle and a fourth round pick . the studs continue to indicate that they're tearing down their roster . a new regime appears to be going down that way .

title: ESPN's final points-league mock draft for 2026
content: Default rosters include seven starters along with a C, 1B, 2B, 3B, SS, three outfielders and a utility player . hitters score one point for every base reached via hits (total bases), as well as each walk, run, RBI and stolen base .

title: 'Criminal have to pay soon': Iran's supreme leader on killing of security chief
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: Big 12's top newcomers and what their coaches think
content: the big 12 football landscape is being reshaped by 2026 newcomers . the list includes some of the most high-profile transfers in the country . this breakdown looks at why they matter and how their own coaches and GMs expect them to fit .

title: CFB spring buzz: Bama expectations, Texas upgrades and a freshman QB making waves
content: Alabama coach Kalen DeBoer says it's more than his 20-8 record in the past two seasons there . "we're going to do things the right way," he says in a sit-down interview . he is looking for his first college football playoff appearance with the Trojans .

title: Jay Bilas predicts every game of the 2026 men's NCAA tournament
content: the 2026 men's NCAA tournament is revealed in a bracket . the bracket gives us less than five minutes . my bracket was chalky last season, but the backlash was swift and unrelenting . I was criticized for not picking an underdog to reach Sao Paulo .

title: Power Rankings: Sixth men, rookies and unsung heroes for all 30 teams
content: the Lakers beat the Rockets for their sixth straight win . there's less than a month left in the 2025-26 regular season . teams are already claiming spots in the postseason as well as bowing their way out of any chance of extending their season into June .

title: 'I'm a sucker for a challenge': Why Moneyball pioneer chose to take on Coors Field
content: dePodesta ends a decade-long run with the Cleveland Browns to become the Rockies' president of baseball operations . he wants the rockies to view playing baseball at 5,280 feet above sea level as an advantage, not a hindrance . "this is who we are," he says .

title: Bus Body Code issue: Rahul flags cost burden; Gadkari stresses safety
content: new standards and excessive fees could drive many small manufacturers out of business . road transport minister said tightening safety norms was taken following a spate of bus fire incidents which have claimed lives . he said there is a need for professionalism in building bus and truck bodies in the country .

title: Women's March Madness 2026 bracket: First look at all 68 teams
content: sixty-eight teams will compete in the 2026 women's NCAA tournament . the bracket revealed which teams are in, which bubble teams got left out and which matchups might be waiting on the road to Phoenix, where the national champion will be crowned on April 5 .

title: From LS to assembly poll debut: Why Cong is betting big on Gaurav Gogoi in Assam

title: PM speaks to Kuwait Crown Prince, condemns Iran attacks
content: india's continued engagement with the Gulf saw PM Narendra Modi speak to the Crown Prince . the pm had on Tuesday spoken to the UAE president to condemn the attacks . this was the second time Modi spoke to the crown Prince since the US-Israel strikes on Iran on febr 28 .

title: Two Indian-origin men fined £1,000 each for spitting paan in London
content: two men fined over £1,000 each for spitting paan in the borough of Brent . he failed to appear at his hearing and the case was proved in his absence . the fine is £100 (Rs 12,000) but as he didn’t pay it he was taken to court .

title: 7 China-bound oil tankers now headed for India, says report
content: India-flagged tanker Jag Laadki, carrying crude oil imported from the UAE, arrives at Mundra port in Gujarat on Wednesday

title: Search ops launched in 3 J&K districts; GOC IX Corps visits Kathua
content: security forces launched cordon-and-search operations in three J&K districts . locals reported sighting two terror suspects moving through a rivulet . similar search operations were launched in several villages in doda’s Marmat and Dandi Dhara .

title: Choke on Strait of Hormuz hurts many nations, US is not one of them - in fact, it profits
